tokens: Okay, Joe; I'm going to give you the Nav updates, soon as Jim gets off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I was going to give them some...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, you give it to them.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me go get the TV.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If you're ready to copy, here we go: 280 (heading), 017 (bearing to the LM), 5.5 (distance driven), 3.9(range to the LM), 105, 110 (amp-hours), 090, 090 (battery temperatures) (Pause)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And Motor Temps are both off scale, low.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're up on a slope, Joe, and we're looking back down into the valley and...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And could you give us a frame count...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...when you finish your pan; and, Dave, we'd like one from you.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And if you're still near the Rover, we missed the heading and bearing.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, I'll get you that, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The heading is 270; and the bearing, 017.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And if the Rover's fairly level, we'll give you a Nav update later on.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Give him the readings on the Rover.
Jim, give him the readings on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The shadow (device) is reading 1 to the right.
(Long Pause) And roll is 8 degrees, right.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Man, you all ought to have a great view this time.
Okay, Jim; let's go sample this rock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me take a pan here, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The most beautiful thing I've ever seen.
(Long Pause) Man, we're walking uphill, too!
Very angular, very rough surface texture.
Looks like it's partially...Well, it's got glass on one side of it with lots of bubbles; and they're about a centimeter across.
And one corner of it has got all this glass covering on it; seems like there's a linear fracture through one side.
It almost looks like that might be a contact; it is, within the rock.
It looks like we have maybe a breccia on top of a crystalline rock.
It's sort of covered with glass; I can't really tell.
But I can see a definite linear feature through one side of it which is about a fifth, and the glass covers both sides of what I guess I'm calling a contact.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And there's also - parallel to that contact - one surface which is quite flat, (although) only for about 8 inches or so.
Looks like it's been chipped off.
The boulder itself is on the order of about a meter across and maybe....Gee, it looks like a half meter thick or so.
It's got a fillet up one side, and the other side is in a shadow.
I can't really tell whether - It doesn't look like it's filled.
It's got a fillet on the downslope side, and the upslope side is open and free.
As a matter of fact, it looks like it's almost excavated beneath it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks fairly recent, doesn't it, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It sure does, and I can see underneath the upslope side; whereas, on the downslope side, it (the soil)'s piled up.
Hey, let's get some good pictures of that before we disturb it too much.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...See you crystal clear, and we've got a beautiful tally-ho on you and boulder on the TV.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And it probably is fresh; probably...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...not older than three and a half billion years.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Here sits this rock, and it's been here since before creatures roamed the sea in our little Earth.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We ought to check the dust on the lens of these cameras.
(Pause) And this has just got to be impressive.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (I'll) go up topside here and photo the other side of it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now, I think to not disturb things too much, let's try the fillet first.
(Pause) And then we'll corner the rock.
(Pause) I'm stepping on a piece of glass, right by the tongs.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) See if I can get a bag out.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll get the fillet right here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Stopping Jim so he can get a before photo of the fillet) Wait, wait.
Before you do, let me poke a picture at it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Little beads of glass in there in some places.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, when you lean over downhill, what happens?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now, let's get some typical soil, couple of feet away.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, you know what we're going to do when we get through with this thing, Joe?
We're going to roll it over, and we're going to sample the soil beneath.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I'll take it right out here by the gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It (the soil next to the gnomon) hasn't been disturbed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That might fill a square for the football-size rock.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Get another one, if you can.
(Pause) You can't see with my shadow there very good, can you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Okay; hang on to this one (possibly sample bag 180) for a second.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we have a view of the rille that is absolutely unearthly.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Give me your other bag, Jim; I'll put it in.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Joe) Glad you can enjoy it with us.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Tell me this isn't worth doing, boy.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now we got the fillet, we got the soil; now we need to sample the rock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me get...Give me your hammer.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look at the vesicles in that rock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, listen; I want to get a close-up of that contact.
Hold on to this (either bag 181 or the hammer) a second.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me get my trusty tongs.
As a matter of fact, if you'll pull a bag out, Jim, I'm going to get a quick selected sample here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I've got a little piece of glass right there.
(Pause) If I can get up the hill to it.
(Pause) Think I can put that in there?
(Pause) Okay; don't want to drop that one.
And the soil he will collect with the tongs in just a moment are samples
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I'll grab some soil right there with the tongs; it'll stay.
It seems to be fairly cohesive here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can even dig a trench.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave and Jim, you might want to get some material from the top and under the upslope edge of the boulder.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We'll get it from under...Well, I'm going to roll the boulder over; and I might even roll the boulder down in the rille, Joe.
I got a feeling here (that) we ought to do that.
(Consulting his cuff checklist) We got those.
Now, let me get a close-up.
(Pause) Is my lens too dirty to use?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't see any dust on it at all, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The top of mine is covered with dust, too.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; we'll take Gary's little formula here.
See if we can't get a picture of that contact.
Okay, (move) over a tad (to get the second image of the stereopair).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I go on the other side.
Doesn't that look like a contact to you, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; I think that'll do it.
See if we can't get...Oh, let me take a couple of "after" pictures (of the fillet sample), here, before...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Do you want me to hold one of those (individual sample bags)?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (I'll) stow this sample in your bag (SCB).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And an "after" there, for the material around.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Bring me a couple of bags here, old buddy.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, you're knocking off a few fragments
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Probably the best you're going to be able to do.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: After all that instruction I got...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, I think, up on top here, if you hit it, it will break.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, that rock is really ready to roll!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Breathing hard because of the effort) There's one.
Boy, you ought to see the down-Sun...Ooh!
We got to roll it over and get some of that too.
(Pause) Underneath the rock is, looks like, either glass bubbles or vesicles; I can't tell which because it's in the shadow.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Watch it; I'll go up and get this one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dark black, very fine grain basalt.
(Pause) You got your eye on that one?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, are you going to pick it up or...?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Here, let me get the tongs, and let's get those two.
I was hoping I could get a larger frag here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can get the other one, too, if you want.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, but don't put them both in the same bag.
I'll fold the bag up, and you get the other...Here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, need a number off that bag.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...160, Joe, is for the...(Stops to listen to Joe) Yeah; 160 is for the chip off the corner uphill.
I hope that makes some sense to you, but when you get the pictures back and it's the one that doesn't appear to have any phenos (phenocrysts) in it.
It just looked like a fine-grained basalt, nonvesicular.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now the other one that Jim...Are you getting it?
Here, let me hold the bag for you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: How about the dumbbell fragment there beside it?
You didn't knock that off, did you?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'll show you what I mean.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) No, I think that fell off, Jim.
That looks like the same kind of stuff.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, it fell off when I hit, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But I didn't see it fall off, though.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I didn't either, but I don't think...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks like a different type of rock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Not sure it was there when we started.
(Pause) Okay; let me just look at that one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, we'd like you to finish this sampling and...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...press on with your comprehensive sample, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Lots of glass on it, but can't tell the inside too well.
(Pause) (To himself) Okay; what number is that?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Frag on the top of the rock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me put the hammer back.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If you want, I'll go over and get the...We'll probably going to need the rake for the comprehensive.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, why don't you go get the rake and let me...Let's see; I want to roll the rock over...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If you walk back there, could you see...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...and try to get a picture of the underside, here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...if we have a TV cable hung up on the LCRU someplace?
We are having trouble commanding the...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Looking back at the Rover) Yeah, you do.
I think the wire from the high-gain antenna has got your cable to the TV.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Could you give that unmanned vehicle a little help, please?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause as we briefly see the cable) Done.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To himself) Okay; and roll it over.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The top layer is a breccia.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Probably having knocked his tongs over) Lose my tongs.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Do you want me to bring the other tongs?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing) Let me get them with the scoop.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) A couple of pictures, and we'll get some of that material (that was) underneath the rock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, there's a great big glass bubble on that rock!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...getting a local vertical off the gnomon now.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let me...Okay, pick it up again.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: As soon as you finish this sample, we'd like for you to start on the comprehensive, and we need frame counts.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Jim, get a scoop of that underneath.
Let me go around to the other side and get a picture.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Looks like pristine material, all right.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) (I'm) uphill; I can't get down to you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Meantime, I'm going to configure here for a comprehensive.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, did you want to pick the site here?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, let me get the gnomon out here, and I'll...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughing) That's a good place to put it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: On the bottom of the rock, Joe, it seems to be gray where there's no surface alteration, but there is a surface covering.

And in one portion, there's some glass and almost looks like slickensides (a smooth, sometimes lined, surface created where rack masses in close contact slide across each other) across the glass, and it's about, oh, 4 inches by 4 inches.
And then there's, oh my, one whole corner of that thing that's loaded with glass.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Garbled under Joe) a nice fresh place.
Not by the...Here; right out here.
Think you ought to be able to do some raking there.
(Pause) Okay; I have the picture, cross-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; I'm going to start to rake, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Help me take this clip off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: If I can get over there without falling down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just keep going across in that direction.
(Pause) Joe, the soil is dark gray; and it's fine grain, and I haven't seen any difference in granularity between the LM and our position at all.
It all looks about the same.
It's fairly cohesive with very few fragments in it.
Jim's getting about three or four with each scoopful.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think our...Take one more swath here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Man, we are really up high.
Rolling smooth hills as far as you can see.
And on the near side of the rille as we go up to the north, why, there seems to be quite a bit of debris; whereas in our present position near St.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It (the rille rim and wall below St.
George) might be covered just with a downslope...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...movement (of soil on the flank of Hadley Delta).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, we don't have much for all that raking.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; do you want another swath?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's about, I think, all we can do then.
There's just not that much in there.
(Pause) Boots go in about an inch or so when you press on them.
Packs it down nice and smooth.
Guess you can see the dust jumping up as we walk.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: In the bottom of the Rille over by, I would guess somewhere near the Twins...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Near the Twins (a pair of fresh craters at the edge of the rille near BW/64, about 4.5 km NNW of their current position) I can see several very large boulders.
Very angular, and I guess when I say 'large', they must be 10 meters across.
They're sort of unique in the bottom of the rille - in that particular area.
The other ones look like they're a half the size anyway.
And there does seem to be quite a bit of debris up there along where the Twins are, up on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That one was a little more fruitful...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; let's call it quits there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll press on to...(Looking at CDR-11) We got documented samples.
We'll pick up a double core.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Do you want soil with that comprehensive?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: One bag soil with the comprehensive; and then double core.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Let me picture this here where my big foot went.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, did we get a bag number for the frag?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Prior to hearing Joe) Okay; 187 (will be the soil bag).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get another one (that is, another rake load of soil).
There's a bag; There's a bag.
If you can hold on to this little one (bag 186 with the rock fragments), I'll roll up the big one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; the next thing on the agenda is a double core.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; I'm going to go over and configure for it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Talking a bit louder to Houston than to Jim) Hey, Joe, we've got a crater that looks sort of fresh up here, oh, a hundred meters or so, looks like, with a fairly fresh rim.
Would you like a double core on the rim of that, or would you like us just to pull it right here?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's a change in albedo on the rim; it's much lighter.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Drive the core right down through the rim.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Playfully) I thought you might say that!
Take some of this stuff (meaning the rake samples) back (to the Rover), and we'll have to walk up there.
But that won't take too long.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You were just checking up on us.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And I'll just come up there with you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Come on up here, and I'll get the cores out (of Dave's SCB).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let's head up to the crater.
Think we can get there without any trouble?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The first one right here, you mean?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I was thinking of that bright one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That'll probably take a good 5 minutes to get up there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Joe, I guess we'd take 5 minutes to get up there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Drive the core where else you think might be convenient.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, we've got a good place here.
We've got a fairly deep crater; it must be about 10 meters across, and a meter and a half or so deep, and we'll pick the rim of that.
There's a fresh impact crater in the rim anyway, which looks like it pulled out some...Let me get it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Is that a good enough place for you, Jim, right here?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I wouldn't want to go up much farther on this slope.
It's too hard to get up.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let's give it a double core here.
Bet we get them a good double core.
(Pause as Dave hops up to join Jim) Hey, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, you...Here let me...Why don't you turn around this way?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I was just going to grab the cores.
I was going to take a location shot.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hold that for me while I get the cores out.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...the lower one while I get the upper one in (the extension handle).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause as Dave gives the handle to Jim) Hey, Joe, the boulder we just sampled is the only one of its size anywhere to be seen.
There's a fairly fresh crater up a little ways, maybe another half a kilometer or so, but...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Pull that ring off, will you, Dave?
We've got to screw that off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; here's your hammer in your left hand, Jim.
I'll get over here, and we'll get some photographing of this.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get on the other side, yeah.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Be easier to push it in from the uphill side.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave and Jim, as you're getting a double core for us there, we'll be wanting to leave the station in about 10 to 15 minutes.
We'd like only the big (500-mm) camera photographs following this.
I think we're in good shape on everything else.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Is that as far as you can push it, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's as far as I can push it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I got the picture; go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We've got one full core, second core is going in about 2 inches (5 cm) per hammer stroke.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we've got almost a second core.
(Pause) Got another couple of inches to go, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Responding to Joe) No, the boss (Dave) is (the Ironman).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me come up to the hill there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Come on over this way a little.
Oh, it looks like we have some dings on the cap.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Break it and I got the oth(er cap)...Oh, we got to ram it first.
(Pause) Let me put your hammer away.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; why don't you just hold it (the upper section)?
(Pause as Dave pushes the rammer into the upper section several times with his right palm) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Here, hold this (extension handle) and I'll break it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave, we're standing by for a number on the core.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah; the top one is 03, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Can you get it (with the tongs), Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Don't sweat the caps; we got beaucoup caps.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'll let you...God bless it!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If you can hold this one (the upper section), and I'll put the cap on the bottom one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You know, they're a lot harder to get on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Did you) give Joe the numbers on...?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I gave him the top one.
I can't see the...The bottom's too dirty.
(Pause) Rub it off when you get going there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No problem on the bottom one, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...let me give you this (upper section, which Jim takes in his left hand)...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll put this (lower section, which Jim has in his right hand) in your pack.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause as Dave takes the lower section) Hold on to that (extension handle).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Got all sorts of little goodies to do.
Okay; turn right so I can get the rammer.
(Pause as Dave gets the rammer) Okay; that's rammed.
(Pause as Dave restows the rammer) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can put the one that you got (the lower section) in my pack.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You going to try and recover those caps?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: If you could with the tongs, we'd like it back.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hey, those new caps, Joe, with the metal band on them will sure stay on a lot better.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; that one's...I'll hold the core.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Moving to the right and pointing) Right under here.
I can see the edge of it.(Pause) Better use tongs?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Jim, we'd like a stereo pan with your 70-millimeter camera.
Maybe now is a good time to get it.
Or wherever you think is just...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Look at his hands (which are probably very dirty), Joe.
(Laughing) You figure he could take a picture?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You hold this (extension handle, and) I'll put it (the cap) on (the upper section).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Did you get the number of the other one?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (I don't) know what the other one was?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can call Joe when we unload that.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We can take it later, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: It's the middle one in Dave's sample bag.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Turning to Jim) Hold this (extension handle) and let me put the (second) core-tube cap back (on Jim's PLSS).
Why don't you get your stereo pan, and I'll get the big camera out.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If I can get myself back.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, I just hope there's not any dust on the lens (of Jim's own Hasselblad).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Don't forget the rake and that other sample on the boulder.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, we see you're carrying the ignition key there for the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Laughing as he picks up the gnomon) That's right; that little blue thing?
(Pause) Hey, that gnomon is pretty dirty, unfortunately, Joe.
Just no way to keep that color chart clean with all this dust.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Going to have a little moving base on that pan.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You know, a blurred picture isn't much good.
(Pause) (To himself as he gets out the 500-mm camera) Take off the lens cap!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Joe, I'm going to give you...Looks like I got some pretty good contrast looking up to the northwest.
I'll give you the far side of the rille: a vertical and a horizontal.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we'd like about 2 minutes worth of pictures and then think about leaving.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: How's a 250th (of a second exposure) and a (f/)8 look to you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; the first horizontal strip, Joe, is on the upper layer - not layer - (Laughs) upper region of the far side.
I can't really see our A, B, C, D that we thought we might see.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And then about one-third of the way down.

And there's a nice, big, very interesting outcrop over there, which looks like vertical jointing in a big block with a horizontal layer on the top; the block must be, oh, 2 percent of the rille height and it must be about, oh, twice that across, with the layer maybe one-quarter of the height of the block.
And I got a 500 of that.
I'll also take you a 500 vertical in the same area.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we're interested in your climbing aboard now and start back towards the LM.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The camera (frame) count on Dave's (70-mm) camera is 54, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And the camera count on the 500 is 61.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Maybe you can see mine, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: When you come over to strap me in...
transcript.speaker: Irwin
